[[JA:蘇我比咩神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11625202}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Sogahime Shrine | Image = [[File:Sogahimeshrine01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Chiba City|Q170616|WD=Q170616|1=Chiba (city)}}{{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Chiba City)|Q1075869|WD=Q1075869|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Chiba}}{{ill|lt=Soga (Chiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Soga}} 2-chōme 2-7[http://www.sogahime.com/visit#access Access] Retrieved June 27, 2020 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = Sogahime Ōkami
Chiyoharu Inari Ōkami | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sogahime
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Sogahime Shrine''' (そがひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Soga 2-chōme, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Chiba City)|Q1075869|WD=Q1075869|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba City|Q170616|WD=Q170616|1=Chiba (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The principal enshrined deities are Sogahime Ōkami and Chiyoharu Inari Ōkami. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} ({{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Kami|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Ame-no-Koyane Himegami|1=Himegami}}), and {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, Hime Ōkami, and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}). In the medieval period, the Kasuga deities became the center of worship, and the shrine was called Kasuga Daimyōjin. === History === The date of founding is unknown. According to the myths recorded in the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} was on his eastern campaign and attempted to cross from Sagami to Fusa Province, he encountered a violent storm. To calm it, his consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oto Tachibana-hime|1=UNKNOWN}} threw herself into the sea. According to shrine tradition, five women who had accompanied Oto Tachibana-hime also entered the water with her. Of these, only Sogahime, the daughter of a Soga minister, was washed ashore, where she was cared for by villagers, revived, and returned to the capital. Later, when the villagers heard that Yamato Takeru had died on his way back, they built a shrine to console his spirit. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} was deeply moved by this act and appointed the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}} as {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} of the surrounding area. The Soga clan revered {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and Hime Shrine, and it is said that they invited the deities of both shrines and founded Sogahime Shrine. However, there is another tradition: it was Oto Tachibana-hime herself who was washed ashore and revived, and because she said “I have revived (yo-mi-gaeri),” the place name “Soga” is said to have originated from this phrase. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Chiba District|Q5095301|WD=Q5095301|1=Chiba District, Chiba}} Sogahime Shrine,” and is ranked as a minor shrine. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1613|Q6776|WD=Q6776|1=1613}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated a shrine estate of 10 koku. === Shrine grounds === File:蘇賀比咩神社 手水舎.JPG|Temizuya (purification fountain) File:Sogahimeshrine02.jpg|Torii gate === Access === * Get off at {{ill|lt=Soga Station|Q844422|WD=Q844422|1=Soga Station}} on {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Coordinate list}} * [http://www.sogahime.com/ Sogahime Shrine] (official website) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sogahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sogahime Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:shrine dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba]] [[Category:History of Chuo ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Soga clan]] [[Category:千葉氏|社そかひめしんしや]]

[[JA:蘇我比咩神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11625202}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name = Sogahime Shrine | Image = [[File:Sogahimeshrine01.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Chiba City|Q170616|WD=Q170616|1=Chiba (city)}}{{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Chiba City)|Q1075869|WD=Q1075869|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Chiba}}{{ill|lt=Soga (Chiba City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Soga}} 2-chōme 2-7[http://www.sogahime.com/visit#access Access] Retrieved June 27, 2020 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = Sogahime Ōkami
Chiyoharu Inari Ōkami | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Unknown | Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} | Rituals = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Sogahime
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Sogahime Shrine''' (そがひめじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Soga 2-chōme, {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Chiba City)|Q1075869|WD=Q1075869|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba City|Q170616|WD=Q170616|1=Chiba (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|WD=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The principal enshrined deities are Sogahime Ōkami and Chiyoharu Inari Ōkami. Also enshrined are {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, the {{ill|lt=Kasuga deities|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} ({{ill|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane no Kami|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, and {{ill|lt=Himegami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Ame-no-Koyane Himegami|1=Himegami}}), and {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, Hime Ōkami, and {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}). In the medieval period, the Kasuga deities became the center of worship, and the shrine was called Kasuga Daimyōjin. === History === The date of founding is unknown. According to the myths recorded in the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, when {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} was on his eastern campaign and attempted to cross from Sagami to Fusa Province, he encountered a violent storm. To calm it, his consort {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oto Tachibana-hime|1=UNKNOWN}} threw herself into the sea. According to shrine tradition, five women who had accompanied Oto Tachibana-hime also entered the water with her. Of these, only Sogahime, the daughter of a Soga minister, was washed ashore, where she was cared for by villagers, revived, and returned to the capital. Later, when the villagers heard that Yamato Takeru had died on his way back, they built a shrine to console his spirit. {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} was deeply moved by this act and appointed the {{ill|lt=Soga clan|Q909854|WD=Q909854|1=Soga clan}} as {{ill|lt=kuni no miyatsuko|Q2483673|WD=Q2483673|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} of the surrounding area. The Soga clan revered {{ill|lt=Kasuga Taisha|Q714559|WD=Q714559|1=Kasuga-taisha}} and Hime Shrine, and it is said that they invited the deities of both shrines and founded Sogahime Shrine. However, there is another tradition: it was Oto Tachibana-hime herself who was washed ashore and revived, and because she said “I have revived (yo-mi-gaeri),” the place name “Soga” is said to have originated from this phrase. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “{{ill|lt=Shimōsa Province|Q1053695|WD=Q1053695|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|lt=Chiba District|Q5095301|WD=Q5095301|1=Chiba District, Chiba}} Sogahime Shrine,” and is ranked as a minor shrine. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 19 ({{ill|lt=1613|Q6776|WD=Q6776|1=1613}}), {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} donated a shrine estate of 10 koku. === Shrine grounds === File:蘇賀比咩神社 手水舎.JPG|Temizuya (purification fountain) File:Sogahimeshrine02.jpg|Torii gate === Access === * Get off at {{ill|lt=Soga Station|Q844422|WD=Q844422|1=Soga Station}} on {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|WD=Q499071|lt=JR|1=East Japan Railway Company}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Coordinate list}} * [http://www.sogahime.com/ Sogahime Shrine] (official website) == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sogahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sogahime Shrine]][[Category:shrine dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba]] [[Category:History of Chuo ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo ward]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimōsa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Ototachibana-hime]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Soga clan]] [[Category:千葉氏|社そかひめしんしや]]
